twitch_info.title: Don’t die, butterfly! | I wanna be the Butterfly.

message.body: something something invisible blocks salesman
I know you're only a part time fangame player but would you be interested in doing Friday roulette

every Friday myself, Barfyace, Gab, Jqck, and a rotating cast of other people go to delicious fruit and roll 10 random fangames and play through them with Play Online (a.k.a. shared saves and we can see each other)
we roll the games around this time and play them in around an hour from now
you wouldn't need to do anything but show up, download the games, and play them
if you wanted, ofc
joining one week doesn't mean youd have to join every week to be clear
you could also not join this week and join in future weeks
Barfy, Gab, and I usually stream them with a shared chat
yeah, there's a discord server for it I could invite you to and you could join in future weeks
no obligation to join ever, it's just a fun Friday thing
easy stream content
allows us to play games we otherwise wouldn't
we've found some absolutely amazing games we've never heard of before
shoutouts to ONG2
you also wouldn't have an obligation to stay for the full thing to be clear
roulettes range anywhere from like 3-12 hours
sometimes over a few days
it really just depends on the games we roll
like last week we rolled Try a Collab 2, a super large mega collaboration with several hours of content
if it's too hard we play it for a bit but then skip it
the rule is don't judge a book by its cover, but you can judge it by its first page
I'll send you an invite over discord, if you join and see channels referencing a "Barfy Jam" don't worry about it, it's a thing happening at the end of the month that you don't gotta worry about

Yeah, "panther" refers to the species of the "Pantherinae" subfamily of cats. The members of the Pantherinae subfamily are the jaguar, leopard, lion, tiger, snow leopard and clouded leopard

"Tigers appear orange to humans because most of us are trichromatic (or sensitive to all three primary colors). But boars, deer, and other tiger prey are dichromatic and only pick up green and blue light. They’re effectively colorblind to red, like some people. This means that they cannot distinguish between red-orange tones and green tones. So, an orange tiger, to the prey’s detriment, will seamlessly blend into the forest backdrop."
